question: For the `Point2D` class (from Q7) with private `x` and `y` coordinates, overload the stream insertion operator `<<` to enable printing `Point2D` objects directly to `std::cout` in the format "(x, y)".

test_cases.input: Point2D p3; // Default constructor, x and y uninitialized

errors: The default constructor of the `Point2D` class (`Point2D() {}`) leaves its `x` and `y` member variables uninitialized. When an object created with this constructor is printed, it results in undefined behavior, typically printing garbage values.
A robust `Point2D` class should ensure all its member variables are initialized in all constructors to prevent unpredictable behavior.
